faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in New Hyde Park homes?
faqs.answer: New Hyde Park residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, cigarette beetles, flour beetles, and weevils. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, nuts, and dried fruits. Our $350-$550 service includes identification, elimination, and prevention strategies tailored to your specific pantry pest problem.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to pantry pest problems in New Hyde Park?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service to New Hyde Park and surrounding Long Island areas. Our technicians can typically arrive within 24 hours of your call to assess your pantry pest situation and begin treatment immediately, preventing further contamination of your stored foods.

faqs.question: What does your pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection of your kitchen and pantry areas, identification of infested products, targeted treatment using EPA-approved products safe for food storage areas, removal guidance, and preventative recommendations. We also provide a follow-up visit if needed within our service guarantee to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: We recommend storing dry goods in airtight containers, regularly cleaning pantry shelves, checking expiration dates, and inspecting new groceries before storage. Our technicians will provide personalized prevention tips during your service visit and can set up quarterly maintenance plans to keep your New Hyde Park kitchen pantry pest-free long-term.
